purpose: FLAGSHIP B4: sync-free early routing. B3 validated the predictor (pre/post-attention router agreement 92.6-93.1% on the real 235B) but added ~94 GPU->CPU syncs/token (.tolist() on the early router), collapsing the CPU's launch run-ahead and giving the win back (0.87-1.07x). B4 removes every ADDED main-thread sync: early-router ids go GPU -> pinned ring via async D2H; a dedicated issuer thread waits on that event off the main thread and issues prefetch copies on its own stream; the main thread keeps exactly the one sync per layer that prefetch-off pays. Registered question: with the predictor proven and the sync tax removed, does early-fetch finally beat the serialized baseline?
frozen_commit: the commit carrying this file (harness committed alongside).

protocol.host: RunPod SECURE H100 class (>=150 GB RAM, >=600 GB volume); on-box link microbench.
protocol.runs: SMOKE (--layers 4, paired off/early2, 8 tokens) gates the full build; then full 94-layer: per prompt (3 fixed), OFF (64 greedy tokens) then EARLY2 (128), same process, state reset between.
protocol.reduction: median s/token per mode; agreement A reported.

frozen_verdict_criteria.F1_identity: EARLY2 greedy tokens identical to OFF for the paired prefix, all 3 prompts.
frozen_verdict_criteria.F2_paired: median tok/s(EARLY2) >= 1.05 x tok/s(OFF) on every prompt.
frozen_verdict_criteria.F3_waterfall: tok/s(EARLY2) >= 0.80 x the on-box waterfall ceiling on every prompt.
frozen_verdict_criteria.REPORT: agreement A; absolute tok/s; off-baseline (now replicated on 3 pods at 4.30-4.33); texts + distinct-2.
frozen_verdict_criteria.no_tune_clause: Failures reported at full volume; claims narrowed; no re-runs until green.
